questions.question: Explain the meaning of rights.
questions.answer: Rights are reasonable claims of individuals that are recognized by society and protected by law. They ensure freedom, equality and dignity for citizens.

questions.question: Explain why rights are necessary in a democracy.
questions.answer: Rights are necessary in democracy to protect citizens from misuse of power and to ensure equality, freedom and justice.

questions.question: Explain the Right to Equality.
questions.answer: The Right to Equality ensures that all citizens are equal before the law and prohibits discrimination on the basis of religion, race, caste, sex or place of birth.

questions.question: Explain the Right to Freedom.
questions.answer: The Right to Freedom provides freedoms such as speech, expression, movement, residence and profession to citizens.

questions.question: Explain the Right against Exploitation.
questions.answer: This right prohibits human trafficking, forced labour and child labour to protect individuals from exploitation.

questions.question: Explain the Right to Freedom of Religion.
questions.answer: This right allows citizens to practice, profess and propagate their religion freely.

questions.question: Explain Cultural and Educational Rights.
questions.answer: These rights protect the culture, language and education of minority groups and allow them to establish institutions.

questions.question: Explain the Right to Constitutional Remedies.
questions.answer: This right allows citizens to approach courts if their Fundamental Rights are violated.

questions.question: Explain the role of courts in protecting rights.
questions.answer: Courts enforce Fundamental Rights and provide justice when rights are violated.

questions.question: Explain secularism in India.
questions.answer: Secularism means the state treats all religions equally and does not favor any religion.

questions.question: Explain untouchability and its abolition.
questions.answer: Untouchability is discrimination based on caste. The Constitution abolished this practice to ensure equality.

questions.question: Explain the concept of human rights.
questions.answer: Human rights are universal rights that protect the dignity and freedom of individuals.

questions.question: Explain Public Interest Litigation.
questions.answer: Public Interest Litigation allows citizens to approach courts in matters of public interest.

questions.question: Explain the functions of the National Human Rights Commission.
questions.answer: The NHRC protects and promotes human rights and investigates violations.

questions.question: Explain the Right to Information.
questions.answer: The Right to Information allows citizens to obtain information from government authorities.

questions.question: Explain the Right to Education.
questions.answer: The Right to Education ensures free and compulsory education for children up to 14 years.

questions.question: Explain the Right to Life.
questions.answer: The Right to Life guarantees protection of life and personal liberty.

questions.question: Explain human trafficking.
questions.answer: Human trafficking refers to the buying and selling of people and is prohibited by law.

questions.question: Explain why rights expand over time.
questions.answer: Rights expand as societies develop and new needs arise.

questions.question: Explain the importance of democratic rights.
questions.answer: Democratic rights ensure freedom, equality and justice for citizens.

questions.question: Explain how rights protect minorities.
questions.answer: Rights protect minority groups from discrimination and ensure equal opportunities.

questions.question: Explain why courts are important in democracy.
questions.answer: Courts protect citizens' rights and ensure justice.

questions.question: Explain the role of education in democracy.
questions.answer: Education increases awareness about rights and responsibilities.

questions.question: Explain how RTI improves democracy.
questions.answer: RTI promotes transparency and accountability in government.

questions.question: Explain the relationship between democracy and rights.
questions.answer: Democracy requires rights to ensure equality, freedom and justice for all citizens.
